[śóß]
Variant of Ťáľ .
The reformative usage in simplified Chinese and Japanese that replaced Ťąä can be analysed either as from an elimination of ťõą with glyphic alteration from Ťáľ to śóß or as using a homophonic character.

[Ťąä]
Phono-semantic compound ( ŚĹĘŤĀ≤ / ŚĹĘŚ£į , OC *…° ∑…Įs ): semantic ťõą ( ‚Äú owl ‚ÄĚ ) + phonetic Ťáľ ( OC *…°u Ē ) .
Originally refers to a type of owl; later phonetically borrowed for a homophonous word meaning "old." The upper portion frequently appears as ŤźĎ (a type of grass).
